scope: Process-noise-adaptive UKF (PUKF): rule and thresholds predeclared before evaluation on the controlled force-model-mismatch slice.
estimator: PUKF -- holds measurement noise R fixed at the nominal vector and inflates the process noise Q multiplicatively when a sliding-window estimate of the R-only normalized innovation squared (NIS) suggests innovations are dominated by a dynamics/force-model bias rather than measurement noise.
rationale: Classical statistical-OD intuition (Mehra 1970; Jazwinski 1970; Stacey & D'Amico 2021): when the prior is wrong (force-model bias), Q should grow; when the measurement is wrong (noise inflation), R should grow. The AUKF in this manuscript adapts R only; the PUKF is its symmetric counterpart.

rule.windowed_statistic: R-only NIS averaged over the last window_size visible updates
rule.decision: if windowed_mean_NIS / expected_NIS >= nis_alarm_ratio then q_scale_target = q_scale_alarm; elif windowed_mean_NIS / expected_NIS >= nis_warn_ratio then q_scale_target = q_scale_warn; else q_scale_target = 1.0
rule.step_independence: The Q-scale at step k uses only the windowed NIS computed from updates strictly before step k, so the predict step does not see step-k innovations.

thresholds_justification: Expected R-only NIS per visible update is the chi-square mean for the 4-channel measurement model (E[chi2(4)] = 4). Round half-integer ratios (1.5, 2.0) and round multiplicative inflation factors (3, 10) are chosen a priori; smoothing 0.4 is a standard innovation-adaptive-filter forgetting factor.

evaluation_protocol.data: Controlled force-model mismatch slice (truth inflates ballistic coefficient, drag density, SRP, process-noise std, C_R; estimator keeps nominal compact model).
evaluation_protocol.primary_endpoint: Observed-step position RMSE on the same trajectory population as the AUKF mechanism diagnostic.
evaluation_protocol.decision_rule: PUKF is a positive contribution if and only if its mean observed-step RMSE beats every fixed-noise/AUKF comparator and the paired-bootstrap 95% CI for the PUKF-minus-best-comparator gap is strictly negative (PUKF strictly better).
evaluation_protocol.honest_negative: If the predeclared rule does not yield a positive in the sense above, it is reported as a bounded negative (no rule retuning is allowed).
